story: Jamaican-British singer Carl Douglas kicked his way into the world on May 10, 1942. Born in Kingston, Jamaica, Douglas would later become famous for his catchy disco hit 'Kung Fu Fighting'. This funky tune had people everywhere doing karate chops and high kicks on dance floors around the globe!
activity_question: What was Carl Douglas famous for?
activity_choices: His catchy disco hit 'Kung Fu Fighting'
His ability to do high kicks
activity_answer: His catchy disco hit 'Kung Fu Fighting'
follow_up_question: Can you think of any other songs that are inspired by sports or fighting styles?

title: From Rock Star to TV Composer
story: Jay Ferguson rocked his way into music history. Born in Burbank, California in 1947, he first gained fame with the band Spirit. Their hit 'I Got A Line on You' made waves. Later, Jay's solo career soared with 'Thunder Island'. But on May 10, he was born to become more than a rocker. He'd eventually compose the catchy theme for the popular TV show 'The Office'!
activity_question: What TV show did Jay Ferguson compose the theme for?
follow_up_question: Can you hum or sing part of The Office theme song?

title: Yodeling Queen of Swedish Country
story: Kikki Danielsson burst onto the music scene with a unique talent. Born in Osby, Sweden in 1952, she became a star who could yodel, play accordion, and sing both pop and country tunes. Her powerful voice and musical skills made her stand out. On May 10, Kikki's birthday, fans celebrate the artist who brought yodeling to Swedish pop!
activity_question: What two genres of music did Kikki Danielsson sing?
follow_up_question: Have you ever tried yodeling? It's a fun vocal technique!

title: Frontiersmen Fight Back in Bacon's Rebellion
story: Angry settlers clashed with the government in Virginia! On May 10, 1676, Bacon's Rebellion erupted as frontiersmen took up arms. They were fed up with unfair treatment and lack of protection from Native American attacks. Led by Nathaniel Bacon, the rebels stood up to the colonial rulers, demanding change and better representation for common folks.
activity_question: Who led the rebels in Bacon's Rebellion?
follow_up_question: Why were the frontiersmen angry with the Virginia government?

title: America's First Naval Giant Sets Sail
story: Cannons boomed as the USS United States slid into the water. This massive warship, launched in 1797, marked the birth of America's naval power. Built to protect merchant ships, she was the first of six frigates ordered by Congress. With her sturdy oak hull and powerful guns, the United States stood ready to defend the young nation on May 10.
activity_question: What was the USS United States built to protect?
bonus_fact: The USS United States was nicknamed 'Old Wagon' for her size and strength.

title: America's Birthday Bash Begins
story: Crowds cheered as the Centennial Fair kicked off in Philadelphia. This massive celebration marked America's 100th birthday on May 10, 1876. Visitors marveled at new inventions like the telephone and typewriter. The fair showcased how far the young nation had come and sparked dreams of future progress.
activity_question: What was the Centennial Fair celebrating?
bonus_fact: The fair's main building was the largest in the world at the time.
